title: Bracketed paste: paste multi-line text as one input, not many
task:
Pasting multi-line text into the norn-tui input field today treats each line as a separate user input — the terminal delivers the pasted content as a stream of key events, every newline in the paste becomes an Enter, every Enter dispatches Submit, and the agent receives line 1 / line 2 / line 3 as three turns instead of one multi-line message. The fix is bracketed paste mode: tell the terminal to wrap pasted content in ESC[200~ … ESC[201~ and handle the resulting termina Event::Paste(String) by inserting the text into the editor with embedded newlines preserved as buffer newlines (NOT as Submit).

OUTCOME — what the user experiences after the fix:
- Pasting a 10-line block puts all 10 lines into the input editor as a single multi-line draft. The editor grows to fit, the cursor lands at the end of the pasted content, no agent turn is dispatched.
- Each embedded newline becomes an editor newline (same effect as pressing Alt+Enter), not an action submission.
- Pasting while the autocomplete popup is open dismisses the popup (the pasted text isn't a popup filter) and the paste lands in the editor.
- Pasting inserts at the cursor, splicing around any surrounding existing text rather than appending.
- Exiting the TUI restores the terminal's previous bracketed-paste state — same hygiene we already apply to Kitty flags and DECSTBM.

requirements.description: Enable bracketed paste during TerminalGuard initialisation. In crates/norn-tui/src/terminal/setup.rs the `new()` function already emits Kitty PushFlags conditionally on caps.kitty_keyboard; emit `Csi::Mode(Mode::SetDecPrivateMode(DecPrivateMode::Code(DecPrivateModeCode::BracketedPaste)))` (or the termina-0.3 equivalent — DecPrivateMode::BracketedPaste = 2004) unconditionally after raw mode is entered. Bracketed paste is supported by every modern terminal (Terminal.app, iTerm2, Ghostty, Kitty, WezTerm, Alacritty, xterm) and falls through harmlessly on terminals that don't grok it. Restore on drop in `cleanup_handle` (reset the mode alongside the existing cleanup writes).
requirements.acceptance: Cargo test in setup.rs (or a new terminal/paste.rs) asserting the init byte stream contains the BracketedPaste DEC private SET escape and the cleanup byte stream contains the matching RESET. The test does not require a real terminal — it writes through an in-memory writer or inspects the captured init sequence. cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

requirements.description: Add a Paste handler in the event loop. crates/norn-tui/src/app/event_loop.rs currently matches Event::Key and Event::WindowResized in two places — the outer input handler (around the `match event` near line 354) and the mid-turn handler (around line 535). Add an `Event::Paste(text)` arm to BOTH that: (a) dismisses any open autocomplete popup, (b) inserts each character of `text` via `state.input_editor.insert_char(ch)` and each '\n' via `state.input_editor.insert_newline()` (NOT InputAction::Submit), (c) calls the existing `sync_input_for_current_geometry(state, guard)` helper, (d) repaints via render_input. Do NOT route the paste through `apply_edit_action` — that path matches single-key actions; the paste should be a direct insertion sequence.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test (preferably under app/event_loop.rs tests or a new app/paste.rs tests module) that synthesises a multi-line Event::Paste and asserts: editor.lines() reflects the pasted line split, editor.text() equals the pasted text, the agent turn count did NOT increment, the autocomplete popup state is None after the paste. Manual gate: cargo test -p norn-tui passes.

requirements.description: Honour cursor position on splice. The paste should insert at the current cursor position, splitting any surrounding text correctly. If the cursor is in the middle of an existing line (say 'hello|world' with cursor at the `|`), pasting 'PASTED\nLINE2' produces lines = ['helloPASTED', 'LINE2world'] with the cursor parked after 'LINE2' on the second line. The existing insert_char + insert_newline methods already handle this if called in sequence — verify and add a test.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test: editor with 'hello world', cursor at column 5 (between 'hello' and ' world'), Event::Paste('PASTED\nLINE2') → editor.lines() == ['helloPASTED', 'LINE2 world'] and editor.cursor_position() points at the end of 'LINE2' on row 1 (row=1, col=5).

requirements.description: Reset bracketed paste on TUI exit. Match the existing cleanup pattern (Kitty flags PopFlags, scroll region reset, cursor restore). In the `cleanup_handle` function in setup.rs add a DEC private RESET for BracketedPaste alongside the existing teardown writes.
requirements.acceptance: Same test as R1's cleanup-side assertion. The TUI does not leave the host terminal in bracketed-paste mode after exit — copy-pasting into the post-exit shell prompt behaves normally (no ESC[200~ wrapper).

context:
FILES:
- crates/norn-tui/src/terminal/setup.rs — TerminalGuard::new() (around line 78), cleanup_handle() (around line 354). Match the existing pattern that gates Kitty PushFlags on caps.kitty_keyboard but emit BracketedPaste unconditionally (it's universally supported).
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/event_loop.rs — outer `match event` near line 354, mid-turn `match event` near line 535. Both need an Event::Paste arm.
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/render.rs — sync_input_for_current_geometry already exists; reuse it from the paste handler.
- crates/norn-tui/src/input/editor.rs — insert_char / insert_newline are the existing primitives.

TERMINA SUPPORT (verified):
- termina-0.3.2/src/event.rs:29: `Paste(String)` variant of Event
- termina-0.3.2/src/escape/csi.rs:1089: `BracketedPaste = 2004` in DecPrivateModeCode
- The init incantation is roughly `\x1b[?2004h` and the cleanup is `\x1b[?2004l` — use the typed termina escapes, not raw strings.

WHAT'S ALREADY CORRECT — don't re-do:
- Raw mode entry
- Kitty PushFlags (separate; do not touch)
- DECSTBM scroll region setup
- The render path that fires after sync_input_for_current_geometry

STANDARDS — per /Users/tom/Developer/ablative/yggdrasil/CLAUDE.md: no unwrap/expect/panic/todo in production code; pedantic clippy clean; no TODO/FIXME/HACK; files under 500 LOC excluding tests; thiserror in library code.

VERIFICATION: cargo check -p norn-tui; cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p norn-tui; grep TODO/FIXME/HACK in changed files.
